absolute.rationale: Absolute tolerances are allowed for exact small closed-form values and result-shape regression checks.

relative.rationale: Relative tolerance is allowed for large restrained thermoelastic force or stress magnitudes.

sign.rationale: Sign checks are allowed only for qualitative direction claims paired with numeric magnitude checks.

field_overrides.rationale: Heat flux is pinned tightly because the two-element gradient is exactly hand-checkable.
Thermal stress uses relative tolerance because the expected restrained magnitude is large.
Thermal axial force uses relative tolerance because the expected restrained magnitude is large.

promotion_rule: Do not reuse this tight closed-form policy for higher-dimensional or mesh-sensitive operators without a separate convergence or reference-tool policy.
remaining_gate: Retain release-run regression output and provenance together before raising manifest entries to qualification.
